session.assistant: The functions have been renamed but `plan_helpers` still exists as a test utility module (used in 48 test files). Let me check if the `import-conflict-resolution.md` doc references are about `parse_plan_number` as a function name - which has been renamed:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Merge Conflict Resolution for Import Consolidation 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:30 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "resolving merge conflicts during rebase" 7→ - "fixing import conflicts after consolidation" 8→ - "rebasing after shared module changes" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Merge Conflict Resolution for Import Consolidation 12→ 13→When import paths are consolidated into shared modules, rebasing other branches can cause merge conflicts. This guide covers the resolution strategy. 14→ 15→## Common Scenario 16→ 17→You consolidated imports in one branch: 18→ 19→```python 20→# Before: multiple local helpers 21→from .local_helpers import parse_url 22→ 23→# After: shared module 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import parse_url 25→``` 26→ 27→When rebasing other branches that still use the old imports, conflicts appear. 28→ 29→## Resolution Strategy 30→
... [105 lines omitted] ...

35→ 36→- `"pr-address:#456:abc123"` -> 456 37→- `"8559:#460:abc123"` -> 460 38→- `"one-shot:#458:abc123"` -> 458 39→- `"rebase:#456:abc123"` -> 456 40→- `"plnd/fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430 (#460):abc456"` -> 460 (plan-implement branch-name format) 41→ 42→The plan-implement workflow uses a `run-name` template of `"${{ inputs.branch_name }} (#${{ inputs.pr_number }}):${{ inputs.distinct_id }}"`, which includes the branch name for easier identification. The `#(\d+)` regex works with both old and new formats because it matches the `#NNN` pattern regardless of surrounding context. 43→ 44→Formats without `#` return None and fall back to plan-PR linkage via `extract_plan_number()` which parses `"NUMBER:HASH"` format. 45→ 46→## Workflow Source Column 47→ 48→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py:42-45 --> 49→ 50→Iterates `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP` (maps command names to .yml filenames) and tags each run with its source workflow. 51→ 52→## Constants 53→ 54→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py:27-29 -->

session.assistant:
There's no `parse_plan_number` function anymore - it's been renamed to `parse_plan_number_from_url`. The import-conflict-resolution doc references `parse_plan_number` as part of a merge-conflict resolution example. This is a teaching document about how to handle conflicts, not a reference to current API. The function names used are illustrative examples of the general pattern. The document is still valid as a pattern guide even though the specific function names in the examples have evolved.
Let me check `extract_plan_number` in workflow-run-list.md:
The reference on line 44 says: `fall back to plan-PR linkage via extract_plan_number() which parses "NUMBER:HASH" format`. Let me confirm this function still exists:

1→"""Shared utilities for run commands.""" 2→ 3→import re 4→ 5→ 6→def extract_pr_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 7→ """Extract PR number from display_title containing '#NNN'. 8→ 9→ Handles: 10→ - "pr-address:#456:abc123" → 456 11→ - "8559:#460:abc123" → 460 12→ - "one-shot:#458:abc123" → 458 13→ - "rebase:#456:abc123" → 456 14→ - "8559:abc123" (old format, no #) → None 15→ - "Some title [abc123]" (legacy) → None 16→ - None or empty → None 17→ """ 18→ if not display_title: 19→ return None 20→ match = re.search(r"#(\d+)", display_title) 21→ if match is None: 22→ return None 23→ return int(match.group(1)) 24→ 25→ 26→def extract_plan_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 27→ """Extract plan number from display_title format '123:abc456'. 28→ 29→ Handles: 30→ - Legacy format: "123:abc456" → 123 (plan_id at start, no #pr_number)
